No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to eradicating poverty or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to ending hunger or improving nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to health, well-being, or related targets.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education focused on academic and business writing, directly providing 'relevant skills for employment' (Target 4.4) and 'advanced academic opportunities' (Target 4.3).
evidence: enhance learners’ academic writing skills to prepare them for more advanced academic opportunities and studies
to be better prepared for the working world

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly aims to prepare learners 'for the working world' and to 'become effective communicators,' directly supporting 'decent work for all' (Target 8.5).
evidence: to be better prepared for the working world
become effective communicators in today’s wired and mobile workplace
develop and raise students’ business awareness

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course includes 'Electronic Message & Digital Media' and prepares students for the 'wired and mobile workplace,' aligning with enhancing ICT use (Target 9.c).
evidence: effective communicators in today’s wired and mobile workplace

Reduced Inequalities.reason: By providing skills for employment, the course indirectly contributes to economic inclusion (Target 10.2), though this is not a primary focus.
evidence: to be better prepared for the working world

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to urban development or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B, 'identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' directly supports adopting sustainable business practices and reporting (Target 12.6).
evidence: understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there is no specific mention of climate change mitigation or adaptation in the syllabus.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Core Competency B, 'identify ethics, social responsibility,' fosters principles essential for accountable and transparent institutions (Target 16.6) and reducing corruption (Target 16.5).
evidence: understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Core Competencies C ('professional communication skills') and E ('teamwork') are foundational for building effective multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.17).
evidence: 溝通能力(Students will acquire professional communication skills.)
團隊合作與領導能力(Students will be able to work effectively in teams as well as develop leadership skills.)
